pages.raw_text:
Spokane will be in the market
with a 81 100000 bridge and water
bond issue before long, as the vot-
ers of the city have authorized the
issuance of bonds to this amount.
The effort to get the approval of
the voters for the proposed
81 100000 issue of bonds for the
extension of the park system of the
city failed to receive the necessary
three-ffths of all votes cast on that
question.


The bond issue authorized con-
sists of $4,000 Of bridge bonds to
build the Monroe street bridge and
complete other bridge building
planned by the city council. These
bonds were approved by a vote of
1924 for, 1857 against.


The 100000 of water extension
bonds, to lift water extension war
rants outstanding and care for the
probable deficit in this department
during the present, has been car
ried by a vote of 1904 for to 1887
agains.


The vote by wards on the park
bonds is; First. for 1889, against
1895; Second. 9S1 for, COL against;
Third. 7sS for. 631 against; Fourth,
1851 for, 1868 against; Fifth, 1894
for, 1853 against.


The new 100000 bond issue
for water and bridges will bring
the bonded debt of the city up to
$4,000
